label: 148 Joint Energetics Transition Office

children.heading: .—In General
children.label: a .—In General
children.text: The Secretary of Defense shall establish a Joint Energetics Transition Office (in this section referred to as the “Office”) within the Department of Defense. The Office shall carry out the activities described in subsection (c) and shall have such other responsibilities relating to energetic materials as the Secretary shall specify.

children.label: A
children.text: report directly to the Under Secretary of Defense for Acquisition and Sustainment; and

children.label: B
children.text: coordinate, as appropriate, with the Under Secretary of Defense for Research and Engineering.

The Under Secretary of Defense for Research and Engineering shall designate an individual to serve as the deputy head of the Office. The Under Secretary shall select such individual from among officials of the Department of Defense serving in organizations under the jurisdiction of the Under Secretary at the time of such designation. The deputy head of the Office shall report directly to the head of the Office and to the Under Secretary of Defense for Research and Engineering.

The head of the Office and deputy head of the Office shall be responsible for the overall management and operation of the Office. The Under Secretaries shall ensure that the head and deputy head of the Office are not assigned outside duties that would diminish their ability to effectively manage and operate the Office.

children.label: A
children.text: developing or supporting the development of strategic plans for energetic materials and technologies, including associated performance metrics for the Office, over the periods covered by the future-years defense program required under and the program objective memorandum process;section 221 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: to determine targets that would be optimally addressed or defeated by weapons that incorporate novel energetic materials; and

children.label: ii
children.text: to inform the program objective memorandum process;

children.label: C
children.text: identifying any shortfalls in the supply chain for energetic materials and developing plans to alleviate any shortfalls through the expansion of the energetic materials industrial base to include critical contractors, subcontractors, and suppliers; and

children.label: D
children.text: identifying raw material waste produced during the explosives manufacturing process and developing plans to reduce waste and optimize production.

children.label: A
children.text: to identify promising new energetic materials and technologies;

children.label: B
children.text: to mature, integrate, prototype, test, and demonstrate novel energetic materials and technologies, including new materials and manufacturing technologies;

children.label: C
children.text: to expedite testing, evaluation, and acquisition of energetic materials and technologies to meet the emergent needs of the Department, including the rapid integration of promising new materials and other promising energetic compounds into weapons platforms;

children.label: D
children.text: to identify or establish prototyping demonstration venues to integrate advanced technologies that speed the maturation and deployment of energetic materials; and

children.label: E
children.text: to support collaboration among industry, academia, and elements of the Department of Defense to transition energetic materials and technologies from the research and development phase to production and operational use within the Department.

children.label: A
children.text: the validation, verification, and accreditation of modeling and simulation of energetic materials for the development of requirements; and

children.label: B
children.text: the qualification process for energetic materials, from discovery through transition to production and integration into weapon systems.
Recommend changes to laws, regulations, and policies that present barriers or extend timelines for the expedited process described in paragraph (3).

Coordinate with other organizations involved in energetic materials activities within the Department of Defense, including the Armed Forces, and across other departments and agencies of the Federal Government.

Pursuant to the authority provided under , establish and manage a Department of Defense Field Activity dedicated to systems engineering associated with energetic materials. Such Field Activity shall be funded under budget activity 3 (advanced technology development) or budget activity 4 (advanced component development and prototypes) (as such budget activity classifications are set forth in volume 2B, chapter 5 of the Department of Defense Financial Management Regulation (DOD 7000.14-R)) to reduce technical risk, integrate research, development, test, and evaluation, and perform system demonstration programs of the Department of Defense on novel energetic materials for use in weapon systems.section 191 of this title
Carry out such other responsibilities relating to energetic materials as the Secretary shall specify.

The Secretary of Defense shall ensure that the Office is budgeted for and funded in a manner sufficient to ensure the Office has the staff and other resources necessary to effectively carry out the responsibilities specified in subsection (c).

In the budget justification materials submitted to Congress in support of the Department of Defense budget for fiscal year 2027 and each fiscal year thereafter (as submitted with the budget of the President under ), the Secretary of Defense shall include a dedicated budget line item for the implementation of subsection (a) and for the testing and evaluation of energetic materials and technologies by the Office.section 1105(a) of title 31
release large amounts of stored chemical energy; and

children.label: A
children.text: create lethal effects in warheads in kinetic weapons components and systems; or

children.label: B
children.text: increase propellant performance in a weapon propulsion system as related to lethal effects, range, or speed.
